free_text: Experimental research to confirm the excitation of
Gamov-Teller resonance in compound nucleus 118Sb
Original text was published in
the journal Yadernaya Fizika i Inzhiniring, 2016,
The neutron detector was situated at a
distance of 1.5 m from the target.
Protons were registered by a telescope with dE
detector thickness from 11-14 mu-m and E detector
thickness of 1100 mu-m.
The resonance cross section was determined by
subtracting the nonresonance component from the total
cross section.
All cross section errors are statistical. Where they
are not indicated they are less than the size of a
data point on a plot.
Entry was approved by Dr. L.N. Generalov
*By author* C-12 was an admixture in Sn-117 target.
First series of measurement
Second series of measurement
From Fig.7 of Phys.At.Nuclei, 80,1539,2017
Data were presented by Dr. L.N. Generalov
The data for Sn in absolute units are obtained with the
use of theoretical values calculated by means of the
optical model with the use of optical potential
parameters, see MONIT-REF.
A self-supporting target with a
thickness of 530 mu-g/cm2 with 89.3% 117Sn was set at
an angle of 45 deg to the proton beam. The energy
thickness of the target was equal to 20-30 keV.
